posts.title: New Watershed Restoration Project Launched in Oromia Region
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has officially launched a major watershed restoration initiative targeting over 15,000 hectares of degraded highland landscape in the Oromia region. The project will engage more than 200 farming communities through participatory soil and water conservation, agroforestry expansion, and community forest management systems.
posts.author: AFOLU Project Management Office

posts.title: Bosona Uummamaa: Reeshoo Uwwisa Bosonaa fi Naannolee Itoophiyaa
posts.body: Odeeffannoon haaraan kutaa Bosona jalatti maxxanfame. Bosonni uummamaa Itoophiyaa yeroo tokko %40 ture, gara %3tti kufe, har'a garuu %23.6tti ol-guddateera. Kana malees, sababa hir'ina bosonaa (population growth, agricultural expansion, overgrazing), fi uwwisa bosonaa naannolee Itoophiyaa — Oromiya (62.5%), SNNP (19.0%), Gambella (13.2%) fi kkf — odeeffannoo guutuu dabalataan jira.
posts.author: AFOLU Forestry Sector

posts.title: 2026 Spring Green Legacy Planting Season Opens Across Six Regions
posts.body: The 2026 Green Legacy Initiative spring planting season has officially begun across six Ethiopian regions, with community mobilization events drawing thousands of participants in the opening week. AFOLU Ethiopia is supporting regional coordination, providing technical guidance on species selection, site preparation, and post-planting care to maximise seedling survival rates.
posts.author: Green Legacy Program Office

posts.title: 2025 Annual REDD+ Carbon Monitoring Report Published
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has published its 2025 REDD+ Carbon Monitoring Report, presenting updated forest cover and carbon stock estimates for priority landscapes. Net carbon sequestration increased by an estimated 4.2 million tonnes CO2 equivalent compared to the 2020 baseline. Participatory forest management communities accounted for over 70% of the conservation outcomes documented.
posts.author: AFOLU Research & Knowledge Management

posts.title: Eight Community Forest Cooperatives Certified in Kaffa Zone
posts.body: Eight community forest management cooperatives in Kaffa Zone, SNNPR, have received official certification from the Ethiopian Forest Development Agency following a rigorous governance and management assessment. The certified cooperatives now have legal authority to manage, protect, and benefit from over 45,000 hectares of Afromontane forest.
posts.author: AFOLU Forestry Sector

posts.title: Homegarden Agroforestry Expansion Reaches 12,000 Households in SNNP Region
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia's agroforestry sector has reached a major milestone with 12,000 smallholder households in the SNNP region now implementing improved homegarden agroforestry systems. The systems integrate indigenous tree species with enset, coffee, and food crops, improving nutrition, household income, and microclimate resilience for participating families.
posts.author: AFOLU Agroforestry Sector

posts.title: Partnership Signed with FAO for Climate-Smart Agriculture Scaling
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has signed a three-year partnership agreement with FAO to scale climate-smart agriculture practices across smallholder communities in Ethiopia's highland and lowland regions. The agreement covers development of context-specific climate adaptation toolkits and the training of over 500 agricultural development agents.
posts.author: AFOLU Executive Office

posts.title: Improved Tef Variety Trials Completed in Amhara and Oromia Highlands
posts.body: Multi-location variety trials for five new drought-tolerant tef varieties have been completed in 18 highland woredas across Amhara and Oromia. Preliminary results show yield gains of 25-40% over local check varieties under reduced rainfall conditions, with two top-performing varieties now recommended for formal release through the national seed system.
posts.author: AFOLU Plant Science Sector

posts.title: 500 km of Hillside Terracing Completed Across Tigray Watersheds
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia's natural resource management team, working alongside regional watershed bureaus and community groups, has completed over 500 kilometres of stone-faced hillside terracing across priority degraded watersheds in Tigray. The work is projected to reduce surface runoff and soil loss by up to 60% in treated areas.
posts.author: AFOLU Natural Resource Management Sector

posts.title: Community Animal Health Worker Training Programme Graduates 320 Paravets
posts.body: A graduation ceremony held in Bahir Dar marked the completion of AFOLU Ethiopia's latest Community Animal Health Worker training cohort. A total of 320 paraveterinary workers from four regions received certification after completing a 12-week curriculum covering livestock disease recognition, vaccination delivery, and first-response treatment protocols.
posts.author: AFOLU Animal Science Sector

posts.title: Farmer Training of Trainers Programme Launches in 25 Woredas
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has launched a Farmer Training of Trainers programme across 25 priority woredas, equipping lead farmers with skills in integrated soil fertility management, climate-smart agronomic practices, and community facilitation. Each trained farmer is expected to provide peer-to-peer extension support to a minimum of 20 neighbouring households.
posts.author: AFOLU Rural Development Sector

posts.title: Ethiopia Coffee Agroforestry Value Chain Assessment Released
posts.body: A new value chain assessment published by AFOLU Ethiopia's agro-economics sector documents the economic performance of shade-grown coffee systems in southwestern Ethiopia. The report finds that farmers in certified agroforestry coffee systems earn on average 38% more income per kilogram compared to conventional coffee growers, driven by premiums from specialty and organic market channels.
posts.author: AFOLU Agro-Economics Sector

posts.title: Community Ecotourism Enterprise Established Near Bale Mountains National Park
posts.body: A new community-based ecotourism enterprise has been formally registered in the buffer zone of Bale Mountains National Park, supported by AFOLU Ethiopia's wildlife and ecotourism programme. The enterprise, managed by a local cooperative of 84 households, will offer guided wildlife walks, traditional homestay accommodation, and cultural exchange experiences to visiting tourists.
posts.author: AFOLU Wildlife & Ecotourism Sector

posts.title: Integrated Food Security Assessment Completed in 10 Food-Insecure Woredas
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has completed a comprehensive food security assessment in 10 chronically food-insecure woredas across the Southern and Oromia regions. The assessment evaluated all four pillars of food security and will inform the design of targeted AFOLU interventions for the next programme cycle.
posts.author: AFOLU Food Security Programme

posts.title: Annual Impact Report 2025 Now Available for Download
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia's Annual Impact Report 2025 is now publicly available. The report documents achievements across all eight technical sectors and four flagship programmes, presenting verified data on seedlings planted, hectares restored, households reached, and carbon outcomes. The report also reflects on challenges encountered and lessons incorporated into programme design.
posts.author: AFOLU Communications Team

posts.title: High-Resolution Forest Cover Map for Southwest Ethiopia Completed
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia's geospatial monitoring team has finalised a new high-resolution (10-metre) forest cover map for the southwest Ethiopian highlands using Sentinel-2 satellite imagery and machine learning classification. The map will serve as the updated baseline for REDD+ reference emission level calculations and community forest management monitoring in the region.
posts.author: AFOLU Geospatial & GIS Unit

posts.title: New Watershed Restoration Field Manual Published in Four Languages
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has released a new Watershed Restoration and Soil Conservation Field Manual, now available in Amharic, Afaan Oromo, Tigrinya, and English. The manual provides step-by-step guidance for community facilitators on site assessment, bund construction, gully rehabilitation, and post-restoration monitoring. Print copies have been distributed to all regional partner bureaus.
posts.author: AFOLU Training & Publications Unit

posts.title: End-of-Project Evaluation Released for Oromia Landscape Restoration Initiative
posts.body: The final evaluation report for AFOLU Ethiopia's five-year Oromia Landscape Restoration Initiative has been published. The evaluation documents 28,000 households adopting improved land management practices, 9,200 hectares of degraded land restored, and measurable improvements in stream flow during dry seasons in treated watersheds.
posts.author: AFOLU Monitoring & Evaluation Unit

posts.title: Afar Rangeland Management Programme Enters Second Phase
posts.body: Following a successful first phase, AFOLU Ethiopia's Afar Rangeland Management Programme has entered its second phase with expanded geographic coverage and a strengthened focus on pastoral community governance systems. Phase 2 will work in 18 kebeles across two zones, incorporating participatory rangeland monitoring tools and seasonal mobility planning support.
posts.author: AFOLU Regional Projects Team

posts.title: National Stakeholder Consultation on Ethiopia's Land Restoration Strategy Held
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia co-organised a major national stakeholder consultation on Ethiopia's updated Land Restoration Strategy in Addis Ababa, bringing together over 200 participants from federal and regional government agencies, research institutions, NGOs, and international development partners. The consultation produced priority recommendations that will be integrated into the revised strategy document.
posts.author: AFOLU Policy & Advocacy Team

posts.title: New Photo Series Documents 2026 Green Legacy Planting Events
posts.body: A new photo series capturing the 2026 Green Legacy planting events across Ethiopia has been added to AFOLU Ethiopia's photo gallery. The collection features 45 images from seven regional planting ceremonies, documenting community participation, nursery operations, and restored landscape sites. Images are available for media use with appropriate attribution.
posts.author: AFOLU Communications Team

posts.title: Farmer Voices Series: Communities Share Land Restoration Experiences
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has launched a new 'Farmer Voices' testimonial series featuring first-hand accounts from smallholder farmers, pastoralists, and forest community members across six regions. The series documents the practical impacts of AFOLU programmes on household food security, income, and landscape health, told in participants' own words and languages.
posts.author: AFOLU Communications Team

posts.title: AFOLU Ethiopia Publishes Updated Organisational Profile for 2026
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has published its updated 2026 organisational profile, documenting the organisation's growth, programme portfolio, geographic reach, and institutional partnerships since its founding in 2019. The profile is available in English and Amharic and is intended for use by partner organisations, government counterparts, and development finance institutions.
posts.author: AFOLU Executive Office

posts.title: AFOLU Ethiopia Launches 2026-2030 Strategic Plan
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia has formally launched its 2026-2030 Strategic Plan, setting out the organisation's priorities, strategic objectives, and resource mobilisation targets for the next five years. The plan was developed through a participatory process involving staff, partners, community representatives, and government counterparts across all regions where AFOLU operates.
posts.author: AFOLU Executive Office

posts.title: Dr. Tigist Bekele Appointed as AFOLU Ethiopia Deputy Director for Programs
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia is pleased to announce the appointment of Dr. Tigist Bekele as Deputy Director for Programs. Dr. Bekele brings 18 years of experience in integrated watershed management, climate-smart agriculture, and community-based natural resource governance. She previously led multi-year restoration programmes in Oromia and Amhara and holds a PhD in Environmental Science from Addis Ababa University.
posts.author: AFOLU Executive Office

posts.title: AFOLU Ethiopia Establishes New Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning Unit
posts.body: As part of its 2026-2030 Strategic Plan implementation, AFOLU Ethiopia has established a dedicated Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL) Unit to strengthen evidence generation, adaptive management, and knowledge sharing across all technical sectors and programmes. The unit will implement a new integrated MEL framework and manage AFOLU's organisational learning systems.
posts.author: AFOLU Executive Office

posts.title: AFOLU Ethiopia Recognised at African Land Restoration Summit
posts.body: AFOLU Ethiopia received formal recognition at the 2026 African Land Restoration Summit held in Nairobi, Kenya, for its contribution to Ethiopia's landscape restoration achievements. The organisation was highlighted as a model of integrated AFOLU programming that successfully links technical expertise, community engagement, and national policy alignment at scale.
posts.author: AFOLU Communications Team
